title: QA: terminate_process_tree shells out to PATH-resolved kill (PATH hijack)
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/tools/bash/kill.rs:35, 42, 57
## Violation
The process killer invokes `Command::new("kill")` three times — resolved via `$PATH`. Because `apply_env_scrub` (policy.rs:143-151) does NOT scrub `PATH`, a malicious PATH entry can shadow `kill` and either fail-open (let the runaway child survive) or escalate. Every shell-out's result is `let _`-discarded.

Quoted (kill.rs:34-42):
```
        let _ = Command::new("kill").args(["-TERM", &neg_pid_str]).output();

        let deadline = Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(2);
        let mut exited = false;
        while Instant::now() < deadline {
            let check = Command::new("kill").args(["-0", &pid_str]).output();
```
## Mandated refactor
1. Replace `Command::new("kill")` with `nix::sys::signal::killpg` / `nix::sys::signal::kill` (libc — no PATH lookup, no fork/exec).
2. Use `nix::sys::wait::waitpid` with `WNOHANG` instead of `kill -0` polling.
3. Propagate signal-call failures up instead of `let _`-discarding.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
